persons.arguments_against: Strongest case it is wrong: the dossier flags single-initial and the surname is common (12 officials, 5 biographies); a stray '1883 J. Langford, Clerk' row even hints at another Langford. Why it fails: the forename 'John' is spelled out in both bio and staff list, the compound office 'Registrar of Supreme Court, and Curator of Intestate Estates' matches near-verbatim (sim 91) and is a single unique Fiji post, and the stint reproduces the bio's exact arc - chief clerk / clerk of the legislative council (1878/1883) then registrar of the supreme court and curator of intestate estates (1884/1886 onward) - all within Fiji, with 4 edition-years co-occurring. A competing John Langford holding both of these specific Fiji offices in sequence is implausible.

evidence_cited.fact: ch. clk. and clk. of legis. coun., Jan., 1878
Chief Clerk and Clerk to the Legislative Council
registr., sup. ct., and curator of intestate estates, June, 1884
Registrar of Supreme Court, and Curator of Intestate Estates

persons.would_overturn: A second John Langford in Fiji also recorded as registrar of the supreme court / curator of intestate estates, or a date conflict in the chief-clerk -> registrar succession.

notes: Single-initial flag mitigated by full forename 'John'; two unique compound Fiji offices match near-verbatim across the bio's documented career.
